prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: Waking up is a consistently underwhelming product with poor user experience and minimal customization options. The abrupt transition from unconsciousness to full sensory awareness lacks a smooth onboarding process, often accompanied by jarring auditory alerts (e.g., alarms) or uncomfortable physical sensations (e.g., stiff neck, dry mouth). Performance is highly variable—some mornings deliver acceptable responsiveness and clarity, while others suffer from significant lag, grogginess, and error messages like “brain not found.” Battery life (i.e., sleep quality) directly impacts functionality, yet the system offers no reliable way to optimize it. Not recommended for users seeking a gentle or predictable start to their day.
